text:
Situation A. How Much Slime is Needed?

  1. How much of each ingredient is needed to make one half of a recipe of slime? What strategies did you use?
  2. Zack packages each recipe in a container. He plans on 2 containers of slime for every 3 children. At this rate, what is the number of containers of slime needed for 30 children?

1. One 4-ounce bottle of washable school glue;

    2. 1/2 teaspoon of baking soda;

    1-1.5teaspoons of saline solution;

    optional: 1 drop of food coloring and 1/8 cup of glitter.

    You can divide each value by 2 since you are making one-half of a recipe. Students may talk about multiplying by 1/2 also, which presents a good opportunity for students to (re)notice that this is the same as dividing by 2.

  2. 2 containers (2 recipes) for every 3 children

    3. ↓ × 10 ↙

    ? containers for 30 children

    20
